title: VulnHunter fix-verification disposition
description: Per-vulnerability fix-verification verdict written by the /vulnhunt-fix-verify skill at <out>/verify_disposition.json on every successful run. The skill is read-only over the target repo; verdicts are based on static reasoning over the supplied checkout. Defined by docs/vulnhunt-fix-verify-design.md §9.1.

schema_version.description: Disposition schema version. Constant for this schema file; consumers fail-closed if it differs.

scan_id.description: Original scan identifier. Equals the results-directory basename: <repo>_VULNHUNT_RESULTS_<model>_<UTC-timestamp>.

target_repo.description: Identifies the fixed-code checkout the verdict was rendered against.

path.description: Absolute path of the fixed-code checkout passed in as `repo`.

head_commit.description: HEAD commit SHA at verify time, read from <repo>/.git/HEAD. Empty string if not derivable via Read (e.g. no .git directory, worktree, unexpected packed-refs format).

head_ref.description: Symbolic ref name (branch/tag) or 'detached'. Empty string if not derivable.

additional_repos.description: Additional read-only checkouts the caller supplied via `additional_repos` on the kickoff prompt. Empty array when none were supplied. Recorded for audit so a downstream reader can tell which sources the verifier was allowed to consult when reaching the verdict. Together with `path`, these form the set of trusted roots.

verified_at.description: UTC ISO-8601 timestamp when the verdict was written.

dispositions.description: One entry per VULN-NNN in the caller's `fixed` argument. Empty array is not valid — at least one finding must be verified.

vuln_id.description: Scan-local finding identifier. Zero-padded 3-digit form (VULN-001), matching scan_manifest.schema.json #/$defs/vuln_id.

verdict.description: Per-finding verdict. FIXED=all applicable gates pass; NOT_FIXED=sink still reachable with attacker-controlled input; PARTIAL=primary instance fixed but sweep or class-elimination gate failed; INCONCLUSIVE=cannot answer from static reasoning; INVALID_INPUT=finding id not in report.

gate_status.description: Outcome of one gate. pass/fail are authoritative; skipped means the gate ran but couldn't reach a verdict; n/a means the gate didn't apply (e.g. sweep_complete when the finding had no sweep group).

claim_status.description: Outcome of evaluating one claim from the comments file per skill-fix-verify/comment_rules.md.

evidence_kind.description: Category of evidence backing a gate result.

rationale.description: Human-readable explanation tying the verdict to specific code observations. Should reference file:line locations rather than generalities. Internal: used by phase 4 and by tooling that wants the short form.

issue_comment.description: GitHub-issue-ready markdown for this finding. Posted verbatim as a comment on the corresponding issue by the downstream integration. Includes the verdict, per-gate outcomes with file:line citations, any regressions, and a one-line trailer naming the verified checkout. Templates per verdict are specified in skill-fix-verify/phases/phase2_verify.md.

gates.description: Per-gate outcome. v1 has four gates; the exploit_test_replay gate from the design's wider plan is deferred (docs §13).

evidence.description: Concrete observations the verifier used. Empty array is allowed for INVALID_INPUT verdicts where no inspection occurred.

location.description: file:line in the FIXED checkout, or 'comments:<line>' when citing the comments file.

detail.description: One-sentence observation. Cite a code construct, sanitizer call, missing escape, etc.

regressions.description: file:line entries that match the original root-cause pattern in the fixed checkout but are not addressed. Populated when sweep_complete=fail.

comments_evaluation.description: Outcome of running the optional comments file through comment_rules.md. Always present (with provided=false and claims=[]) even when no comments file was supplied.

provided.description: True iff the caller passed a `comments:` argument.

claims.description: One entry per claim extracted from the comments file. Empty when provided=false.

excerpt.description: Verbatim text segment of the claim.

rationale.description: Why the verifier reached this status. References the rule (R1-R5) and, where applicable, the file it read.

cited_location.description: file:line cited by the claim, if any. Absent when the claim had no citation (i.e. when status is rejected_unverifiable for lack of a citation).
